content.full_text:
South Montebello Irrigation District
A PUBLIC CORPORATION
864 WEST WASHINGTON BOULEVARD
MONTEBELLO, CALIFORNIA 90640
(213) 721-4735

DIRECTORS
ELMER L. RYERSON
PRESIDENT
FLORENCE E. KELLY
VICE PRESIDENT
ROBERT H. BROWN
DIRECTOR

GEORGE A. HERTZING
ASSESSOR, TAX COLLECTOR AND TREASURER
PATRICIA D. SINCLAIR
CORPORATE SECRETARY, OFFICE MANAGER
THOMAS A. SINCLAIR
GENERAL MANAGER

23 January 1990

California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P O Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

REFERENCE: AB 941, Ch 403, St of 1989, amending Sec 87200
of the Government Code

Gentlemen:

After reviewing the letter of 12 January 1990 from the Los Angeles County
Board of Supervisor's office regarding the above amendments, we have the
following questions:

1. Should the Treasurer of our District be required to file
with the FPPC if he cannot make public investments without the
permission of the Board of Directors of the District. Such in-
structions are entered into the minutes of the District as a direct
Board directive authorizing and directing the Treasurer to make
investments as set out by the Board.

2. Our pension fund is managed by a Pension Committee composed
of two board members and the Secretary of the District. The
funds are deposited with Trust Services of America, which directs
the investments. We also have a consultant, and actuarial agent,
who advises the committee when asked to do so, and aids in prepar-
ing tax returns and other forms required by the Federal Government.
As our pension fund is insured by ERISA. The Secretary of the
District already files a 721 Form with the District. Should she
now file also with FPPC. What about TSA and our actuarial agent.

3. For the benefit of those making new disclosures, we need to
know if "income received" includes salary from employment.

We have replied to the Board of Supervisors, but need the above information,
which we were advised came from your office.

Thank you for your help.

SOUTH MONTEBELLO IRRIGATION DISTRICT

Patricia D. Sinclair
Corporate Secretary

ACWA • ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA WATER AGENCIES • SINCE 1910

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from the South Montebello Irrigation District to the California Fair Political Practices Commission regarding amendments to AB 941, Ch 403, St of 1989, amending Sec 87200 of the Government Code. The District has several questions: 1. Should the Treasurer of their District be required to file with the FPPC if he cannot make public investments without the permission of the Board of Directors of the District? Such instructions are entered into the minutes of the District as a direct Board directive authorizing and directing the Treasurer to make investments as set out by the Board. 2. Our pension fund is managed by a Pension Committee composed of two board members and the Secretary of the District. The funds are deposited with Trust Services of America, which directs the investments. We also have a consultant, and actuarial agent, who advises the committee when asked to do so, and aids in preparing tax returns and other forms required by the Federal Government, as our pension fund is insured by ERISA. The Secretary of the District already files a 721 Form with the District. Should she now file also with FPPC. What about TSA and our actuarial agent. 3. For the benefit of those making new disclosures, we need to know if "income received" includes salary from employment. We have replied to the Board of Supervisors, but need the above information, which we were advised came from your office. Thank you for your help. Patricia D. Sinclair Corporate Secretary
